seo.title: UNILIFT Traction Hoist | UL 1323 Listed Man-Riding Hoist | FAS-Tech
seo.description: UNILIFT motorized traction hoist. UL 1323 listed, man-riding certified, 1,500 to 2,200 lbs WLL, unlimited lift height. Built for the US jobsite. Request a quote.
seo.ogTitle: UNILIFT Traction Hoist | UL 1323 Listed Man-Riding Hoist
seo.ogDescription: Motorized traction hoists for lifting people and equipment. Unlimited height, built for the American jobsite.

productSchema.name: UNILIFT Traction Hoist Range
productSchema.description: Electric wire-rope traction hoist for man-riding and material lifting. UL 1323 listed, CSA Z271 certified, EN 1808:2015. Unlimited lift height.

contact.title: Ready to spec the UNILIFT for your project?
contact.intro: Get a custom quote, request the full technical datasheet, or discuss US distribution.

hero.subtitle: UL and CSA-certified traction hoists with capacities from 1,500 to 2,200 lbs, engineered for the North American suspended access market.

product.intro: UNILIFT is an electric traction hoist built for swing stages, suspended scaffolds, elevator installation, and material lifting. Its continuous wire-rope design pulls the suspension rope through the hoist instead of winding it onto a drum, allowing the lifting height to be determined by the length of wire rope deployed.

points.title: Man-riding certified
points.line: — Designed and certified to UL 1323, CSA Z271 and EN 1808 for lifting people on suspended platforms, with all safety devices built in.
— Three models. Available in single-phase or three-phase configurations, 208 V / 60 Hz.

points.title: Integrated OSLA brake
points.line: — Independent secondary safety device that automatically locks the wire rope if the platform exceeds its rated descent speed.

features.subline: All required safety devices integrated as standard, per EN 1808.

items.imageAlt: Built-in overload mechanism on the UNILIFT hoist
items.title: Built-in Overload Mechanism
items.descriptionHtml: When the load reaches or exceeds the limit, the hoist stops rising and will only descend. Trips at 125% WLL.

items.imageAlt: Manual safety lowering mechanism on the UNILIFT hoist
items.title: Safety Lowering Mechanism
items.descriptionHtml: In a power failure, the electro-mechanical brake can be released manually for a safe, controlled descent.

items.imageAlt: Hour counter in the UNILIFT connection box
items.title: Hour Counter
items.descriptionHtml: An internal running-hours counter in the connection box records operating time, so service intervals are always known.

items.imageAlt: UNILIFT attachment points and suspension options
items.title: Attachment Points
items.descriptionHtml: Suspend from a central load pin, or fix at the housing corners with a hook, screw or pins. Adaptable to any rig.

closeups.heading: Engineered down to the last detail.

items.title: Build quality
items.text: Every UNILIFT is factory-tested before it ships, with E-stop, up/down, overload alarm and status indicators sealed in one IP55 control box.
items.image: assets/Front panel of the UNILIFT traction hoist.jpg
items.imageAlt: UNILIFT control box with E-stop and FAS-Tech nameplate
items.mobileImage: assets/Front panel of the UNILIFT traction hoist-1.jpg
items.mobileImageAlt: UNILIFT control box with E-stop and FAS-Tech nameplate

items.title: The OSLA overspeed lock
items.text: Mounted above the rope inlet, the OSLA secondary brake grips the rope on breakage or overspeed. Fully mechanical and independent, it needs no power to arrest a fall.
items.imageAlt: Close-up of the OSLA overspeed lock at the top of the hoist
items.mobileImageAlt: Close-up of the OSLA overspeed lock at the top of the hoist

items.title: Built to be serviced
items.subline: Connected for the jobsite, designed for uptime.
items.text: Built for demanding jobsites to minimize repairs and downtime. An integrated hour meter supports accurate maintenance records, while the familiar 500-hour overhaul cycle requires no specialized tools.
items.imageAlt: UNILIFT power connector and service connection detail
items.mobileImageAlt: UNILIFT power connector and service connection detail

tabs.label: OSLA Brake
tabs.calloutHtml: Important: The OSLA automatically locks the wire rope on rope breakage or overspeed. It must always remain fitted; the hoist cannot operate without it.

tabs.label: Wire Rope
tabs.calloutHtml: Safety: Use only FAS-Technology authorised 3/8" (9.5 mm) steel wire rope in good condition.

tabs.label: Electrical
tabs.calloutHtml: Note: Electrical connection must comply with applicable national standards; supply voltage and frequency must match the hoist nameplate.

tabs.label: Dimensions
tabs.calloutHtml: Note: Dimensions are nominal. Refer to the drawing and to FAS-Technology for installation-specific values.

items.title: Elevator installation
items.line: Lift crews and materials in the shaft during install, with unlimited height on the rope you deploy.

items.title: Suspended platforms & facade access
items.line: Swing stages and BMU rigs for cleaning, caulking, painting and inspection on high-rise facades.

items.title: Material lifting
items.line: Fast, compact vertical material handling. At 159 lbs the hoist is easy to move site to site.

items.title: Windmill / turbine tower access
items.line: Reliable, repeatable vertical access inside the tower for maintenance crews at extreme height.

faq.intro: Certification, specifications, safety, and support, answered in one place.

items.question: Is UNILIFT certified for use in the United States and Canada?
items.answer:
Yes. North American UNILIFT models are certified to UL 1323 and CSA Z271 for powered suspended scaffold and suspended access applications.

Certification applies to the specified hoist model and configuration. Always verify the certification markings, voltage, capacity, and serial number shown on the equipment nameplate.

items.question: Is UNILIFT certified for lifting people?
items.answer:
Yes. UNILIFT is designed and certified for personnel lifting on powered suspended scaffolds, swing stages, and suspended access platforms.

“Man-riding hoist” is commonly used internationally. In the United States, “personnel-lifting traction hoist” or “powered suspended scaffold hoist” is more conventional.

The complete platform, rigging system, fall protection, and installation must also comply with applicable jobsite requirements.

items.question: Does a UL-certified hoist make my suspended scaffold OSHA compliant?
items.answer:
No single component makes the complete scaffold OSHA compliant.

UNILIFT provides a certified hoist for integration into a properly designed suspended scaffold system. The complete installation must comply with OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart L, applicable state and local requirements, and relevant standards such as ANSI/ASSP A10.8.

The employer, qualified person, and competent person remain responsible for system design, rigging, inspection, training, fall protection, and safe operation.

items.question: Which UNILIFT model do I need: single-phase or three-phase?
items.answer:
UNILIFT is available in three North American configurations:

1,500 lb capacity, 208 V single-phase
1,500 lb capacity, 208 V three-phase
2,200 lb capacity, 208 V three-phase

The single-phase model is suitable for jobsites where three-phase power is unavailable. Three-phase models offer lower current draw and include the maximum-capacity 2,200 lb configuration.

Model selection should consider platform capacity, number of hoists, available power, working height, and cable length.

items.question: What is the maximum lifting height?
items.answer:
UNILIFT has no fixed drum-capacity limit.

The wire rope passes continuously through the traction mechanism instead of winding onto a drum. Practical lifting height is therefore determined by the deployed wire rope, electrical cable, rigging arrangement, and jobsite requirements.

items.question: How large and heavy is the UNILIFT hoist?
items.answer:
The North American UNILIFT weighs approximately 159 lb.

Its nominal dimensions include:

Height: 30.2 inches
Width: 11.8 inches
Wire rope: 3/8 inch
Lifting speed: approximately 35 feet per minute

Refer to the technical drawing before designing mounting brackets, stirrups, or platform interfaces.

items.question: Can UNILIFT replace a Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, or Bisomac hoist?
items.answer:
UNILIFT operates in the same powered suspended scaffold hoist category as Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, and Bisomac equipment.

A direct replacement cannot be assumed. Manufacturers may use different mounting interfaces, wire-rope constructions, electrical connectors, control systems, capacities, and safety devices.

FAS-Tech can review your existing scaffold motor or traction hoist configuration and identify the required UNILIFT model, mounting arrangement, and electrical connection.

items.question: What happens if the suspension rope breaks or the hoist overspeeds?
items.answer:
UNILIFT includes an independent OSLA overspeed safety lock.

If the primary suspension rope fails or the platform exceeds the permitted descent speed, the OSLA automatically grips the independent safety wire rope and stops the platform.

The safety lock is factory-installed and electrically interlocked with the hoist.

items.question: What happens if power fails during operation?
items.answer:
The electromagnetic brake engages automatically and holds the load.

A trained operator can use the manual brake-release system to perform a controlled emergency descent. The procedure must follow the operating manual and should only be performed by personnel trained on the equipment.

items.question: Can I use any 3/8-inch wire rope?
items.answer:
No. Matching the diameter alone is insufficient.

UNILIFT must only operate with FAS-Tech-authorized 3/8-inch wire rope of the specified construction, strength, lay, and condition. Using an incompatible rope can reduce traction, damage the hoist, or prevent the safety system from operating correctly.

Do not reuse rope from a Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, or other scaffold hoist unless FAS-Tech has confirmed compatibility.

items.question: What are the lead times and warranty?
items.answer: Both are confirmed with your quote, based on configuration and quantity. Send us your timeline and we'll come back with dates.

items.question: Can our technicians service UNILIFT themselves?
items.answer:
Qualified technicians can perform routine inspections and maintenance by following the UNILIFT service manual.

The hoist uses maintenance procedures familiar to suspended scaffold technicians and does not require proprietary service tools for standard work. An integrated hour meter supports accurate maintenance records, with routine service intervals and a 500-operating-hour overhaul cycle.

Major repairs and overhauls must be completed by trained and authorized personnel.

items.question: Do you have U.S. distributors and service partners?
items.answer:
FAS-Tech currently supports U.S. customers directly while developing its distributor and service network.

Contact FAS-Tech to confirm available sales, training, spare-parts, and technical-service coverage for your state. Distribution and service territories remain open for qualified suspended-access companies.

Third-party company and product names are trademarks of their respective owners. They are referenced only to identify comparable equipment and compatibility considerations.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: UNILIFT VS TIRAK TRACTION HOISTS
items.answer:
UNILIFT operates in the same powered suspended access category as Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, and BISOMAC traction hoists.

Each manufacturer uses different capacities, wire ropes, mounting interfaces, controls, and safety systems. A competitor hoist should never be replaced without first confirming the complete platform configuration.

Tirak offers one of the broadest traction-hoist ranges in the industry, including electric and pneumatic models from approximately 700 to 5,500 lb. Comparable Tirak XE models include 1,500 and 2,200 lb configurations.

UNILIFT focuses on the capacities most commonly required for North American suspended access: 1,500 lb in single-phase or three-phase and 2,200 lb in three-phase. This focused range supports swing stages, modular platforms, façade access, and elevator installation without requiring a different hoist family for each configuration.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: UNILIFT VS SKY CLIMBER LNX AND COMPACT HOISTS
items.answer:
Sky Climber LNX and Compact hoists are established options for conventional suspended scaffold applications. The LNX range includes capacities up to 1,500 lb, uses 5/16-inch wire rope, and operates at approximately 32 to 35 fpm.

UNILIFT covers the same 1,500 lb application range while adding a 2,200 lb model for heavier platforms and equipment loads. Its 3/8-inch wire-rope system is standardized across the North American UNILIFT range.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: UNILIFT VS SPIDER SC1000 AND SC1500 HOISTS
items.answer:
Spider offers 1,000 and 1,500 lb electric traction hoists for standard suspended access work. Its range also includes battery-powered equipment and the specialized SC1500 GHS, which travels at up to 70 fpm for guided elevator applications.

UNILIFT is designed for conventional 35 fpm suspended access and elevator installation, with capacities extending to 2,200 lb. It is suited to contractors who need higher rated capacity without moving to a specialized high-speed system.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: UNILIFT VS POWER CLIMBER PC3 HOISTS
items.answer:
The Power Climber PC3 range includes 1,000 and 1,500 lb models in 208 V single-phase and three-phase configurations. A 110 V, 1,000 lb version is also available. PC3 hoists use 5/16-inch wire rope and travel at approximately 35 fpm on 208 V power.

UNILIFT begins at 1,500 lb and extends to 2,200 lb. It is positioned for contractors who prioritize higher platform capacity and use 208 V jobsite power rather than requiring a lower-capacity 110 V hoist.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: UNILIFT VS BISOMAC 210 HOISTS
items.answer:
The BISOMAC 210 is available in single-phase and three-phase configurations with capacities up to 1,500 lb. It is known for low-voltage performance, side-cover service access, and simplified maintenance.

UNILIFT provides a comparable 1,500 lb range while adding a 2,200 lb three-phase model. An integrated hour meter supports accurate service records, and standard maintenance can be performed using familiar suspended scaffold hoist procedures and conventional workshop tools.

items.category: COMPARE UNILIFT WITH LEADING SUSPENDED SCAFFOLD HOISTS
items.question: WHICH TRACTION HOIST IS RIGHT FOR YOUR APPLICATION?
items.answer:
The correct hoist depends on more than rated capacity. Selection should consider:

Available jobsite voltage and phase
Total suspended platform load
Number of hoists
Wire-rope specification
Mounting and stirrup interface
Lifting speed
Control-system compatibility
Required safety devices
U.S. or Canadian certification
Spare-parts and service availability

FAS-Tech can review your current Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, or BISOMAC configuration and recommend the appropriate UNILIFT model and integration requirements.

Competitor names and trademarks belong to their respective owners. References are provided solely to identify comparable product categories and assist with equipment selection.

manufacturing.heading: How every UNILIFT traction hoist is produced
manufacturing.subline: A semi-automated production line combines manufacturing efficiency with controlled assembly and inspection. Standardized processes improve consistency, production capacity, and reliability across every UNILIFT traction hoist.

seo.title: About Us | FAS-Technology
seo.description: FAS-Tech develops certified traction hoists and suspended access equipment for contractors, elevator installers, and façade access professionals worldwide.

story.heading: Built for contractors who work at height
story.lead: FAS-Tech develops certified traction hoists, suspended access equipment, and lifting solutions for contractors, rental companies, elevator installers, and façade access professionals worldwide.
story.body: We combine European engineering, controlled manufacturing, and practical jobsite experience to deliver equipment that is safe, robust, and straightforward to maintain.
story.support: Our support extends beyond the product. From equipment selection and project engineering to technical documentation, training, spare parts, and after-sales service, we help customers keep their crews working and their projects moving.

market.label: North America
market.text: FAS-Tech provides UL and CSA-certified solutions designed around local power supplies, platform configurations, and jobsite requirements.

story.imageAlt: Technician on a suspended access platform above a city skyline

values.heading: What drives us
values.intro: Three principles guide how we design equipment, support crews in the field, and stand behind every UNILIFT traction hoist.

items.label: Certified safety
items.line: Equipment tested and certified to applicable North American and European standards, supported by documented quality controls and production inspections.

items.label: Jobsite uptime
items.line: Robust equipment designed for demanding environments, familiar maintenance procedures, standard tools, and straightforward access to service parts.

items.label: Responsive support
items.line: Application guidance, technical documentation, training, troubleshooting, and after-sales assistance throughout the equipment lifecycle.

certifications.heading: Tested to the standards that govern suspended access
certifications.intro: Safety and regulatory compliance are built into every UNILIFT traction hoist. Our North American and European models are tested and certified to the standards governing powered suspended access equipment in their respective markets.

items.description: U.S. safety standard for portable hoists used on suspended scaffolds and swing stages. Covers construction, electrical safety, mechanical performance, braking systems, and operational reliability.
Canadian standard governing the design, manufacture, testing, and safe operation of suspended access equipment.
European models comply with the essential health and safety requirements of the Machinery Directive and carry CE marking for placement on the European market.
European standard for suspended access equipment, covering design, construction, stability, safety devices, controls, testing, and protection against mechanical and electrical risks.
